The problem occurs with signaling NaN (SNaN) values. When x is SNaN (#xfc80), the original code returns SNaN, but the transformed code returns a different SNaN (#x7c80). The issue is that fcmp ole with SNaN should return false (since SNaN is unordered), so the select chooses the original x value (SNaN), but fabs canonicalizes SNaN to a quiet NaN or changes its sign bit. The transformation is only valid when we can guarantee no NaN values or when the function has nnan (no NaN) fast math flags. I need to find where this transformation is implemented and add a check for NaN handling.
